texto: What's funny about the slacker thing, people project an image of what they think a musician is: young, slack, unemployed - like a really romantic idea of a poet, writer or musician - which isn't really true a lot of the time. I don't reckon you would know anything about me if I wasn't moderately hard-working.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Courtney Barnett: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “What's funny about the slacker thing, people project an image of what they think a musician is: y…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, time, romantic pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e romantic.

frase_original: What's funny about the slacker thing, people project an image of what they think a musician is: young, slack, unemployed - like a really romantic idea of a poet, writer or musician - which isn't really true a lot of the time. I don't reckon you would know anything about me if I wasn't moderately hard-working.

texto: I really want a Christmas in New York one year, when it's snowing. Like, it's Christmas morning, and you have a fight with someone, and you run down the street, and it's snowing, and you can't find them.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Courtney Barnett: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “I really want a Christmas in New York one year, when it's snowing. Like, it's Christmas morning, …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sucesso, morning, fight pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de bom-dia e natal.
